title: Add Signed Integers
description: Add integers with the same or different signs by combining magnitudes and determining the sign from the larger magnitude.
condition: An expression of the form a + b where a and/or b are signed integers.
response: If signs are the same, add magnitudes and keep the sign; if different, subtract magnitudes and keep the sign of the number with greater absolute value.
label: Principle / Model

title: Compute Position from Signed Rate and Time
description: Calculate position by multiplying signed rate by time, interpreting the sign of the product as direction.
condition: Given a rate with sign (e.g., ±r meters/second) and a time t, and asked for position relative to a reference point.
response: Compute r · t and interpret the sign of the product as direction (positive or negative).
label: Principle / Model
example: If a train travels at $10$ m/s for $-5$ seconds relative to a meeting time, compute $10 \cdot (-5) = -50$, meaning $50$ meters in the negative (south) direction.

title: Compute Rate as Change Over Time
description: Calculate a constant rate by dividing the change in a quantity by the elapsed time.
condition: A situation gives an initial value, a final value, and elapsed time.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Includes interpreting vertical position as signed numbers.

title: Determine Sign of Product or Quotient
description: Use sign rules to determine whether a product or quotient is positive or negative.
condition: Multiplication or division of two signed numbers.
response: State whether the result is positive or negative based on sign rules.
label: Concept
notes: Rule: same signs → positive; different signs → negative.

title: Divide by a Fraction Using Its Reciprocal
description: Rewrite division by a fraction as multiplication by its reciprocal.
condition: Expression of the form $a \div \frac{b}{c}$ where $a, b, c$ are rational numbers and $b/c \neq 0$.
label: Principle / Model

title: Find Time from Rate and Distance
description: Determine elapsed time by dividing total change in position by a known constant rate.
condition: A constant rate and total change in quantity are given; time is unknown.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Equivalent to solving a multiplication equation with rate × time = change.

title: Identify Sign of a Fraction
description: Determine whether a fraction is positive or negative from signs of numerator and denominator.
condition: A fraction with signed numerator and/or denominator.
response: Classify the fraction as positive or negative.
label: Concept
notes: Closely related to sign rules for division.

title: Interpret Account Balance Meaning
description: Explain the meaning of a positive or negative account balance in context.
condition: Given a signed account balance in a money context.
response: State whether the person has money or owes money and by how much.
label: Concept
notes: Focus on conceptual interpretation, not computation.

title: Interpret Context as Signed Number
description: Translate contextual phrases (e.g., below sea level, withdrawal, debt) into positive or negative numbers.
condition: A real-world quantity described relative to a reference level (e.g., below sea level, debt).
response: Represent the quantity with an appropriate signed number.
label: Concept
notes: Sign convention depends on reference level.

title: Interpret Net Change from Signed Sum
description: Interpret the sign and value of a total signed sum as overall increase or decrease in context.
condition: A real-world context modeled by a sum of signed numbers producing a total.
response: State whether the result represents a net increase or decrease and by how much.
label: Principle / Model
example: $-3 + 6 + 2 - 5 + 1 - 4 = -3$, so there are 3 fewer books at the end of the day.

title: Multiply or Divide Signed Numbers
description: Determine the sign and magnitude when multiplying or dividing two signed rational numbers.
condition: Expression involving multiplication or division of two signed rational numbers.
response: Multiply or divide absolute values and apply sign rules (same signs → positive; different signs → negative).
label: Principle / Model
example: $-5 \cdot (-11) = 55$ because the product of two negatives is positive; $-20 \div (-4) = 5$.

title: Multiply Signed Decimals
description: Multiply decimal numbers and correctly place the decimal point and sign in the product.
condition: Expression involving multiplication of signed decimals.
response: Multiply as whole numbers, place the decimal based on total decimal places, and apply sign rules.
label: Principle / Model
example: $(3.6)(-0.2) = -0.72$ because $36 \cdot 2 = 72$ and there are two decimal places total.

title: Represent Descending Motion with Negative Rate
description: Model downward movement using a negative rate in an equation.
condition: A situation describes descending or downward motion relative to a reference level.
response: Assign a negative value to the rate or change.
label: Concept
example: Descending 2.5 ft/s is represented as −2.5 ft/s, leading to \(−2.5x = −20\).
notes: Sign convention depends on defining upward as positive.

title: Represent Direction with Signed Numbers
description: Use positive and negative numbers to represent opposite directions in a context.
condition: A contextual situation involving opposite directions (e.g., north/south) that must be represented numerically.
response: Assign one direction as positive and the opposite as negative, and represent quantities accordingly.
label: Rule / Plan
example: Let north be positive and south be negative. Then $10$ m/s north is $+10$, and $7$ m/s south is $-7$.
notes: Choice of which direction is positive is conventional.

title: Solve One-Step Multiplication Equation
description: Solve equations of the form px = q by dividing both sides by p.
condition: Equation of the form px = q, where p ≠ 0.
response: Divide both sides by p to get x = q/p.
label: Principle / Model

title: Subtract Signed Numbers
description: Rewrite subtraction as addition of the opposite and compute the result.
condition: Expression of the form a - b where a and/or b are signed rational numbers.
response: Rewrite as a + (-b) and compute the sum.
label: Principle
notes: Also used in expressions like $y - x$.

title: Translate Rate Situation to Multiplication Equation
description: Represent a constant-rate situation with an equation of the form px = q.
condition: A word problem describes constant rate and total change with unknown time.
response: Write an equation rate × time = total change.
label: Principle / Model
example: Descending 2.5 ft/s for x seconds to reach −20 ft: \(−2.5x = −20\).
notes: Requires identifying variables and consistent sign conventions.

title: Update Account Balance Sequentially
description: Update a running total by adding each transaction amount to the previous balance.
condition: Given prior balance B and transaction amount t.
label: Principle
